     Part VIII
OS Internals Mastery




         261
Professional Engineering Series                                 Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




Introduction: The Invisible Manager
Dost, aapne kabhi socha hai ki jab aap `printf("Hello World");` likhte ho, toh woh screen par
kaise dikhta hai?     Hardware (monitor, CPU, RAM) toh sirf bits samajhta hai.     In bits aur
aapke high-level code ke beech ka bridge "Operating System" hai.




10.768          1.1 What an Operating System Is?
OS aik aisi software layer hai jo hardware resources ko manage karti hai aur users/applications
                                  Core Denition: OS ek Resource Manager hai (CPU,
ko ek convenient interface deti hai.
Memory, I/O devices ko baant-ta hai) aur ek Extended Machine hai (Hardware complexity
ko hide karta hai).




10.769          1.2 User Space vs Kernel Space
Yeh concept sabsse zaroori hai. CPU ke do modes hote hain:


    User Mode (User Space): Jahan aapki normal apps (Chrome, VS Code) chalti hain.
       Yahan resources restricted hote hain.


    Kernel Mode (Kernel Space): Jahan OS ka core chalta hai. Isse hardware par poora
       control hota hai.


   Agar app ko le read karni hai, toh use User Space se Kernel Space mein switch karna
padta hai via   System Calls.

10.770          1.3 Types of Operating Systems
  1.   Batch OS: Purane zamane ke, jahan tasks queues mein chalti thin.
  2.   Time-Sharing OS: Modern OS jaise Linux/Windows jahan multiple users/tasks CPU
       share karte hain itni fast ki lagta hai sab saath chal raha hai.


  3.   Real-Time OS (RTOS): Jahan timing critical hai (e.g., Robot control, Flight systems).

10.771          System-Level Intuition
OS darsal hardware ka "Guardian" hai. Agar har app ko direct RAM ya HDD ka access mil
jaye, toh aik buggy program poore computer ko crash kar sakta hai. OS "Isolation" provide
karta hai.




10.772          Debugging Techniques
Jab koi program crash hota hai "Segmentation Fault" ke sath, toh darsal OS ne use stop kiya
hota hai kyunki woh aisi memory access karne ki koshish kar raha tha jo uski nahi thi.   Tool:
`dmesg` command Linux mein kernel logs dekhne ke liye use hoti hai.




                                                262
Professional Engineering Series                          Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




             Figure 10.1: User Mode vs Kernel Mode via System Call Interface




                                          263
Professional Engineering Series                               Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




10.773          Elite Engineering Insight: Page Table Optimization
                & TLB Math
Dost, memory management sirf "malloc" nahi, ye hardware search optimization ki math hai.



10.773.1 1. Virtual Memory & Page Tables
OS RAM ko virtualize karta hai. Har process ko lagta hai uske paas 128TB memory hai. Isko
manage karne ke liye    Page Tables ka structure O(1) lookup ke liye hierarchical (Multi-level)
hota hai.



10.773.2 2. TLB (Translation Lookaside Buer)
CPU memory access ke waqt page table scan nahi kar sakta (too slow). Isliye woh aik special
hardware cache use karta hai jise    TLB kehte hain. Math: Agar TLB cache-hit rate 99% hai,
toh memory access       1ns lega.   Agar cache-miss hai, toh "Page Walk"   100ns lega.   Senior
engineers code likhte waqt   Spatial Locality maintain karte hain taaki TLB misses minimum
hon.



10.773.3 3. Memory Alignment & Huge Pages
Large databases (like Oracle/Postgres) 4KB pages ki jagah     Huge Pages (2MB or 1GB) use
karte hain taaki page table entries kam hon aur TLB overhead minimize ho jaye.




10.774          Exercise
  1. Aik program likho jo `getpid()` system call use karke apna process ID print kare.


  2. Calculate the memory overhead of a page table if each entry is 8 bytes and we have 1
       Million pages.


  3. Kyun User Mode apps direct CPU registers (like control registers) ko modify nahi kar
       sakti?




Common Mistakes
Developers aksar ignore karte hain ki System Calls "expensive" hoti hain. User space se Kernel
space mein jana transition overhead leta hai.    Isliye 1-1 byte karke le read karne ki bajaye
"Buered I/O" use karna chahiye.




Introduction: The Blueprint of the Kernel
OS Architecture decide karti hai ki kernel kitna bada hoga aur hardware ke kitne kareeb hoga.
Yeh design performance, stability aur security ko eect karta hai.




                                               264
Professional Engineering Series                                Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




10.775           2.1 Monolithic Kernels (The Giant)
Sabsse common design (Linux, Windows).        Poora OS services (File system, Network stack,
Drivers) single binary mein Kernel space mein chalti hain.

      Pros: Super fast communication (no context switching within services).
      Cons: Ek driver crash hua toh poora OS crash (Panic/Blue Screen).


10.776           2.2 Microkernels (The Minimalist)
Sirf zaroori cheezein (IPC, basic scheduling, memory management) kernel mein hoti hain.
Drivers aur File systems User Space mein chalti hain as "Servers".

      Pros: Highly stable aur secure. Driver crash karega toh bas driver restart hoga, OS nahi.
      Cons: Performance lag kyunki har service ke liye Context Switching/IPC chahiye.


10.777           2.3 Hybrid Kernels
Monolithic aur Microkernel ka mixture (macOS/XNU). Performance ke liye kuch services kernel
space mein rakhi jati hain magar design microkernel jaisa hota hai.




10.778           Architecture Insights: Layers vs Rings
CPU hardware levels (Rings) implement karta hai: -     Ring 0: Kernel (Full access). - Ring 3:
User Apps (Restricted). OS design in rings ko use karta hai process isolation enforce karne ke
liye.




10.779           Real-World Examples
-   Linux: Monolithic. - L4: Microkernel. - Windows NT: Hybrid design approach.

10.780           Debugging Techniques: Kernel Panics
Jab kernel crash hota hai, toh woh `Registers` aur `Stack Trace` dump karta hai.         System
engineers `kdump` ya `serial console` debuggers use karte hain crash analysis ke liye.




10.781           Exercise
1.   Agar aap aik medical device bana rahe hain jo "Critical" hai, toh aap Monolithic prefer
karenge ya Microkernel? Kyun? 2. Kyun driver development Monolithic kernels mein mushkil
hai?




Common Mistakes
User-Kernel boundaries ka ehsas na hona. Har system call aik transition cost laati hai. High-
performance software mein hum "User-mode drivers" (like SPDK/DPDK) use karte hain bypass
karne ke liye.


                                               265
Professional Engineering Series                                  Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




Introduction: Programs in Motion
Program sirf ek le hai disk par (ELF, EXE). Jab OS use memory mein load karke run karta
hai, toh woh    Process ban jata hai. Process aik "Active Entity" hai.

10.782          3.1 Process Creation: fork() and exec()
Unix systems mein process kaise bante hain:


   1.   fork(): Current process ka exact copy (Child) banata hai.
   2.   exec(): Child process memory ko naye program se "replace" kar deta hai.
Windows mein `CreateProcess()` use hota hai jo direct naya program load karta hai.




10.783          3.2 Process Life Cycle (The States)
Aik process ki life in states se guzarti hai:


    New: Process ban raha hai.

    Ready: RAM mein hai, CPU ka intezar kar raha hai.

    Running: CPU par execute ho raha hai.

    Waiting/Blocked: I/O (disk/network) ka intezar kar raha hai.

    Terminated/Zombie: Kaam khatam ho gaya magar parent ne abhi status check nahi
        kiya.




10.784          3.3 Process Control Block (PCB)
OS har process ke liye ek data structure rakhta hai (PCB): - Process ID (PID). - Program
Counter (PC). - CPU Registers. - Memory Limits. - List of open les.




10.785          System-Level Intuition
Process darsal ek "Sandboxed Environment" hai.           OS use memory aur CPU deta hai aur
monitor karta hai ki woh apni hadaan (boundaries) cross na kare.




10.786          Debugging Techniques: ps, top, strace
- `ps -aux`: Saare running processes dekhne ke liye.       - `strace -p <pid>`: Ek process konsi
system calls kar raha hai, live dekhne ke liye.       Sabsse powerful debugging tool for systems
engineers!




10.787          Exercise
1. Python/C mein ek program likhein jo `fork()` kare aur child PID print kare. 2. "Zombie
process" kya hota hai aur isse system ko kya nuksan hota hai?


                                                266
Professional Engineering Series                          Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




         Figure 10.2: Memory Layout: Process (Shared) vs Threads (Private Stack)




                                          267
Professional Engineering Series                                 Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




Common Mistakes
"Fork Bomb" banana.      Bina limits ke processes create karna system resources (PID space,
Memory) ko exhaust kar deta hai. Hamesha `ulimit` set karein.




Introduction: Unit of Execution
Process aik bhari (heavyweight) entity hai. Agar humein aik hi program mein multiple tasks
saath karne hon (jaise Web server mein har request ke liye ek handler), toh hum    Threads use
karte hain.




10.788         4.1 Threads vs Processes
      Process: Apna separate address space (RAM) hota hai. Safe hai magar communication
       (IPC) slow hai.


      Thread: Same process ki memory share karte hain. Fast communication magar "Unsafe"
       (Race conditions ka darr).


Analogy: Process aik poori "Company" hai, Threads us company ke "Employees" jo same
oce aur les share karte hain.




10.789         4.2 Thread Lifecycle & Stacks
Har thread ka apna: - Stack (Local variables). - Program Counter. - Registers. Magar Heap
(Global variables/Memory) shared hota hai.




10.790         4.3 Concurrency Challenges: Race Conditions
Jab do threads ek hi variable ko saath modify karte hain:

count ++; // Machine level : load count , add 1, save count

Agar Thread A ne 'load' kiya aur tabhi context switch ho gaya, toh 'count' ki value corrupt
ho jati hai. Isse solve karne ke liye hum Module 8 mein "Synchronization" seekhenge.




10.791         Architecture Insights: User vs Kernel Threads
-   User Threads: Thread library (like Pthreads) manage karti hai. OS ko nahi pata threads
ke baare mein. Fast magar poor scheduling. -Kernel Threads: OS kernel ko threads ka pata
hota hai. Agar ek thread block ho jaye, toh OS doosre ko schedule kar sakta hai.




10.792         Debugging Techniques: gdb, valgrind
- `gdb` mein `info threads` switch processes/threads debug karne mein help karta hai. - `valgrind
tool=helgrind` data races detect karne ke liye use hota hai.




                                               268
Professional Engineering Series                                   Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




10.793            Exercise
1. C program likhein jo 2 threads create kare aur dono milkar ek global variable ko 1 million
baar increment karein.        Kya result hamesha 2 million aata hai?   Kyun nahi?   2.   Multi-core
CPU aur Hyper-threading mein kya farq hai?




Common Mistakes
"Shared mutable state" ko protect na karna. Threads ko safely use karne ke liye hamesha Locks
ya Atomic operations use karein. Global variables threads ke liye dushman ho sakte hain.




Introduction: The Arbiter of Time
CPU ek mehnga resource hai. Agar 100 processes hain aur 4 cores, toh OS ko decide karna
hai: "Agli 10ms ke liye CPU kiska hai?". Isse     CPU Scheduling kehte hain.

10.794            5.1 Scheduling Objectives
-   Fairness: Har process ko CPU mile. - Throughput: Maximum tasks per hour khatam hon.
-   Turnaround Time: Process start se end tak ka waqt minimum ho. - Response Time:
Pehli baar CPU milne ka waqt (Interactive apps ke liye critical).




10.795            5.2 Algorithms
     1.   First-Come, First-Served (FCFS): Simple queue. Bad throughput (Convoy eect).
     2.   Shortest Job First (SJF): Jo task sabsse chota hai use pehle chalao. Optimal turnaround
          time magar impossible to predict task length exactly.

     3.   Round Robin (RR): Har process ko xed "Time Quantum" (e.g., 20ms) do. Fairness
          ke liye best hai.

     4.   Priority Scheduling: Important tasks (e.g., kernel tasks) pehle chalte hain.


10.796            5.3 Preemptive vs Non-Preemptive
      Non-Preemptive: Process tabhi CPU chodta hai jab woh kaam khatam kare ya I/O
          walla wait kare.

      Preemptive: OS jab chahe process se CPU chheen (preempt) sakta hai (e.g., higher
          priority task aa gaya). Modern OS preemptive hote hain.




10.797            Architecture Insights: Multi-Level Feedback Queue
                  (MLFQ)
Real-world OS (Linux/macOS) aik complex algorithm use karte hain (MLFQ). Yeh process ka
behavior "learn" karte hain. Agar koi process CPU bohot zyada use karta hai, toh uski priority
kam kar di jati hai. Agar koi interactive hai, toh use upar rakha jata hai.


                                                 269
Professional Engineering Series                         Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




                 Figure 10.3: CPU Scheduling GANTT Chart Visualization




                                         270
Professional Engineering Series                                 Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




10.798        Debugging Techniques: top, htop, sar
- `top`: Dekhne ke liye kaunsa process CPU "hog" kar raha hai. - `NICE` value: Aap process
ki priority change kar sakte hain (low nice = high priority).




10.799        Exercise
1. Explain "Priority Inversion" problem (jab low priority task ne lock pakda ho aur high priority
task wait kare). 2. Agar Time Quantum bohot chota ho (e.g., 1ms), toh CPU performance
par kya asar padega? (Hint: Context Switching cost).




Common Mistakes
CPU-bound apps ko "Low Priority" na dena. Heavy processing apps (like video encoding) ko
niche rakha jata hai taaki Mouse/Keyboard response (UI) smooth rahe.




Introduction: The Illusion of Multitasking
Dost, CPU toh ek hi instruction ek waqt mein chala sakta hai (per core). Hamein jo lagta hai
ki 50 apps saath chal rahi hain, woh darsal   Context Switching ka kamaal hai. CPU itni fast
switch karta hai ki insaani aankh ko pata nahi chalta.




10.800        6.1 Saving and Restoring State
Jab OS ek process (A) se doosre (B) par switch karta hai, toh use do cheezein karni hoti hain:


  1.   Save State (Process A): CPU ke registers, Program Counter, aur Stack Pointer ko
       Process A ke PCB (Process Control Block) mein save karna.


  2.   Load State (Process B): Process B ke PCB se purani values utha kar CPU registers
       mein dalna.




10.801        6.2 What Triggers a Context Switch?
Context switch tab hota hai jab:


    Multitasking: Time quantum khatam ho jaye (Round Robin).

    Interrupt: Hardware (keyboard/mouse/disk) ko foran attention chahiye.

    System Call: Process khud waiting state mein chala jaye (e.g., waiting for user input).


10.802        6.3 The Performance Cost
Context switching "free" nahi hai. Isme darsal koi productive kaam nahi ho raha, sirf OS ka
management overhead hai.


    Pure Overhead: PCB save/load karne mein CPU cycles zaya hoti hain.

                                               271
Professional Engineering Series                          Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




               Figure 10.4: Saving and Restoring PCB during Context Switch




                                          272
Professional Engineering Series                               Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




    Cache Pollution: Sabsse bada cost! Jab naya process aata hai, toh CPU cache (L1/L2)
     purane data se bhari hoti hai. Naye process ko baar-baar RAM se data uthana padta hai
     jab tak cache "warm up" na ho jaye.




10.803        System-Level Intuition
Sochiye aap ek book padh rahe hain (Process A). Phone bajta hai (Interrupt). Aap book mein
marker rakhte hain (Saving PC), book band karte ki (Saving state), aur phone uthate hain
(Process B). Phone ke baad aap wapis marker wali jagah se shuru karte hain. Marker hi aapka
PCB hai.




10.804        Debugging & Optimization
System performance check karte waqt hum `vmstat` use karte hain.       Command: `vmstat 1`
Agar `cs` (context switches) column ki value bohot zada hai (lakhon mein), toh iska matlab hai
ki aapke threads apas mein bohot zada switch kar rahe hain, jo system ko slow kar raha hai.




10.805        Exercise
1. Explain karo ki "Thread switch" kyun usually "Process switch" se fast hota hai? (Hint:
Memory space). 2. "Thrashing" kya hoti hai aur iska context switching se kya talluq hai?




Common Mistakes
Bohot zada threads create karna. Agar aap 1000 threads chalayenge ek 4-core CPU par, toh
CPU zada waqt "Switching" mein guzarega bajaye "Execution" ke. Isse     Thread Contention
kehte hain.




Introduction: Processes Talking to Each Other
Dost, security ke liye har process apni "Duniya" (Address Space) mein isolated hota hai. Aik
process doosre ki memory ko touch nahi kar sakta. Magar kabhi-kabhi inhe data share karna
padta hai (jaise Browser aur Print process). Iske liye hum   IPC (Inter-Process Communi-
cation) use karte hain.

10.806        7.1 Shared Memory (The Fast Lane)
OS RAM ka ek tukda dono processes ke beech share kar deta hai.


    How it works: Dono processes ke virtual addresses same physical memory ko point
     karte hain.


    Pros: Fastest IPC (Direct ram access).

    Cons: Synchronization developer ko handle karni padti hai (Locks needed).




                                             273
Professional Engineering Series                                Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




10.807          7.2 Message Passing (The Safe Way)
Processes OS ke zariye messages bhejte hain.

    Pipes: Standard output ko doosre ke input se jodna (`ls | grep txt`).

    Queues: OS ek queue maintain karta hai jahan ek process push karta hai aur doosra
      pop.


    Signals: Chote alerts (jaise `Ctrl+C` jo `SIGINT` bhejta hai).


10.808          7.3 Sockets: Communicating over Network
Agar processes do alag alag computer par hain (ya local bhi), toh Sockets use hote hain. Yeh
TCP/IP stack ka use karte hain.




10.809          Architecture Insights: Sync vs Async IPC
    Synchronous: Sender tab tak wait karta hai jab tak Receiver data na lele (Blocking).

    Asynchronous: Sender data bhej kar apna kaam jaari rakhta hai (Non-blocking).


10.810          Real-World OS Example: Android Binder
Android mein background services aur apps apas mein       Binder IPC se baat karti hain. Yeh
shared memory aur RPC (Remote Procedure Call) ka advanced mix hai.




10.811          Debugging Techniques
Linux mein `ipcs` command se aap active shared memory aur message queues dekh sakte hain.
`strace` se aap dekh sakte hain kab process `read()`, `write()`, ya `shmget()` call kar raha hai.




10.812          Exercise
1. Linux command line par `cat le.txt | grep "error"` chalayein. Yahan IPC kaise kaam kar
raha hai? 2. Shared Memory aur Pipes mein sabsse bada dierence kya hai security ke point
of view se?




Common Mistakes
IPC memory leak. Shared memory ko kaam khatam hone ke baad "Detach" aur "Delete" karna
bhul jana. Yeh system reboot tak RAM consume karti rahegi.




Introduction: Managing the Chaos
Jab multiple threads/processes shared data modify karte hain, toh "Race Condition" paida
hoti hai.   Synchronization woh discipline hai jo ensures karti hai ki shared resources ki state
"Consistent" rahe.


                                               274
Professional Engineering Series                               Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




10.813         8.1 The Critical Section Problem
Code ka woh hissa jahan shared resource (variable, le) access ho raha ho, use   Critical Section
          Requirement: Sirf ek thread aik waqt mein critical section mein hona chahiye
kehte hain.
(Mutual Exclusion).

10.814         8.2 Mutexes and Locks
Mutex (Mutual Exclusion) ek "Key" ki tarah hai. - Agar Key mere paas hai, main room mein
jaunga.   - Aapko wait karna padega jab tak main wapis aa kar key na de doon.          Code (C):
`pthread_mutex_lock(&lock); ... critical code ... pthread_mutex_unlock(&lock);`




10.815         8.3 Semaphores: The Manager
                                Binary Semaphore: Mutex jaisa (0 ya 1). - Counting
Semaphore ek integer counter hai. -
Semaphore: Multiple resources ke liye (e.g., 5 connections allowed). Operations: `wait()`
(decrement) aur `signal()` (increment).




10.816         8.4 Atomic Operations
Kuch operations direct CPU hardware level par atomic (uninterruptible) hote hain.       Example:
`lock cmpxchg` (Compare and Swap).        Modern lock-free data structures inhi par built hote
hain. Performance ke liye yeh Mutex se behter hain kyunki context switch nahi hota.




10.817         Architecture Insights: Spinlocks
Agar critical section bohot chota hai, toh Thread ko "Sleep" karana (context switch) mehnga
hai.   Iske bajaye Thread ek loop mein "Spin" karta hai key check karne ke liye.        Warning:
Spinlocks high CPU use karte hain, sirf kernel drivers mein zaroori hote hain.




10.818         Debugging Techniques: Thread Sanitizers
Google ka `ThreadSanitizer` (TSan) use karein compiler ags ke sath (`-fsanitize=thread`). Yeh
codes mein chupi hui race conditions ko runtime par dhoond leta hai jo normal testing mein
nahi dikhti.




10.819         Exercise
1. "Spinlock" aur "Mutex" mein se kaunsa kab use karna chahiye? 2. Aik program likhein
jahan 2 threads shared array mein data add karein using a Mutex to prevent corruption.




Common Mistakes
"Check-then-Act" error.   Bad Code: `if (count < 10) count++; ` Agar do threads ne line 1
saath check ki, toh dono increment kar denge! Hamesha poore block ko lock karein.




                                              275
Professional Engineering Series                                Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




Introduction: The Eternal Standstill
Dost, Deadlock woh situation hai jahan: - Thread 1 ne Lock A liya hai aur Lock B mang raha
hai. - Thread 2 ne Lock B liya hai aur Lock A mang raha hai. Dono ek doosre ka wait karte
rahenge hamesha ke liye. Program "Hung" ho jayega.




10.820           9.1 The Four Coman Conditions
Deadlock sirf tab hota hai jab yeh 4 cheezein saath hon:

     1.   Mutual Exclusion: Resource share nahi ho sakta.
     2.   Hold and Wait: Ek resource pakda hua hai aur doosre ka intezar hai.
     3.   No Preemption: OS kisi se zor-zabardasti resource nahi chheen sakta.
     4.   Circular Wait: A waits for B, B waits for C, C waits for A.

10.821           9.2 Deadlock Prevention & Avoidance
-   Lock Ordering: Sabsse simple solution. Hamesha locks ko same order mein len (e.g., Always
Lock A then Lock B). No Circular Wait possible! -Banker's Algorithm: OS check karta hai

                                               276
Professional Engineering Series                              Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




ki resource dene se system "Unsafe State" mein toh nahi chala jayega.




10.822        9.3 Deadlock Detection & Recovery
Modern general-purpose OS (Windows/Linux) deadlocks ko prevent nahi karte (overhead bohot
hai).   Woh deadlocks hone dete hain.                                   1. Kill Process:
                                          Par agar system freeze ho jaye:
Deadlock todne ke liye aik process ko kill karna. 2.   Resource Preemption: Kuch systems
rollback support karte hain state ko piche lejane ke liye.




10.823        System-Level Intuition
Sochiye do dost aik hi pencil aur paper share kar rahe hain.   Ek ne pencil li hai aur paper
maang raha hai, doosre ne paper liya hai aur pencil maang raha hai. Agar dono "Ziddi" hain
toh kaam kabhi nahi hoga.




10.824        Debugging Deadlocks
- `gdb` mein `thread apply all bt` (backtrace) command lagayein. Aapko dikh jayega kaunsa
thread kis lock line par phasa hua hai. - Linux mein `/proc/sys/kernel/hung_task_timeout_secs`
check karein, OS hung task detect karke log mein bata deta hai.




10.825        Exercise
1. Circular wait ko todne ke liye "Lock Ranking" ka concept samjhayo. 2. Kyun starvation
aur deadlock alag alag cheezein hain?




Common Mistakes
Nested locks lena bina order ke. Kabhi bhi aisi library function na call karein lock ke andar
jo khud koi unknown lock lene ki koshish kare (Callback functions logic).   Isse deadlocks ke
chances badh jate hain.




Introduction: Managing the Ocean of Bytes
Memory OS ka sabsse precious resource hai. OS ko ensure karna hai ki har process ko RAM
mile, woh safe rahe, aur computer speed mein chale.




10.826        10.1 Physical vs Virtual Memory
-   Physical Memory: Real RAM jo motherboard par lagi hai.         -   Virtual Memory: Ek
illusion jo OS har process ko deta hai. Process ko lagta hai uske paas 2GB (ya zada) memory
hai, par darsal OS use chote chote tukdon mein RAM ke alag hisson mein t karta hai.




                                              277
Professional Engineering Series                               Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




10.827         10.2 Dynamic Memory Allocation
Program mein hum `malloc()` ya `new` use karte hain. Background mein OS do techniques use
karta hai:


      Segmentation: Code, Data, aur Stack ko alag alag segments mein rakhna.          (Purana
       tareeka).


      Paging: Memory ko x-sized blocks (Pages) mein baant-na (Modern tareeka).


10.828         10.3 Fragmentation: The Scattered Trash
-Internal Fragmentation: Block allot kiya 100kb, use kiya 10kb. Beech ka 90kb zaya. -
External Fragmentation: Saari free memory phaili hui hai chote chote tukdon mein. Total
memory hai 1GB, par koi bhi continuous 100MB ka block nahi mil raha.




10.829         10.4 Memory Protection
OS register base aur limit levels use karta hai. Agar process apne allotted area se bahar "Peek"
ya "Poke" karega, toh hardware hardware     Segmentation Fault signal bhejega aur OS use
"Kill" kar dega.




10.830         Architecture Insights: The Page Table
OS ek "Map" (Page Table) maintain karta hai: `Virtual Address -> Physical Address`. Yeh
map CPU ke     MMU (Memory Management Unit) mein rehta hai taaki translation super-
fast ho.




10.831         Debugging Techniques: Valgrind, Free
- `free -m`: System ki total RAM usage dekhne ke liye. - `valgrind leak-check=full <binary>`:
Memory leaks dhoondhne ke liye. Har allocate ki hui memory free honi chahiye!




10.832         Exercise
1.   "Memory Leak" aur "Buer Overow" mein fundamental dierence kya hai?            2.   Kyun
Virtual Memory ke bina multitasking impossible jaisi hoti?




Common Mistakes
"Use-after-free" error. Memory free karne ke baad bhi pointer ko use karna. Isse system crash
ya security vulnerability (Hacking) ho sakti hai. Hamesha pointer ko free karne ke baad `NULL`
karein.




                                              278
Professional Engineering Series                              Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




Introduction: The Great Illusion
Dost, Virtual Memory woh technique hai jisse computer ko lagta hai ki uske paas RAM ke
samandar hain, bhale hi physical RAM sirf 8GB ho. Yeh aik logical abstraction hai jo secondary
storage (HDD/SSD) ka use karti hai RAM ki extension ke taur par.




10.833       11.1 Paging: Breaking Memory into Pieces
OS memory ko x-sized blocks mein baant deta hai:


    Pages: Virtual memory ke blocks (Process ki taraf se).

    Frames: Physical memory ke blocks (RAM ki taraf se).

Sabsse common page size   4KB hota hai.




             Figure 10.5: Mapping Virtual Memory to Physical RAM (Paging)




                                             279
Professional Engineering Series                            Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




10.834           11.2 Demand Paging & Page Faults
OS poora program aik saath RAM mein nahi load karta. Sirf wohi pages load hote hain jin ki
zaroorat hote hain.   Sequence of Event: 1. CPU aisi memory access karta hai jo RAM mein
nahi hai. 2. Hardware OS ko signal bhejta hai: Page Fault! 3. OS disk se woh page uthata
hai, RAM mein dalta hai, Page Table update karta hai. 4. CPU wapis instruction retry karta
hai.




10.835           11.3 Translation Lookaside Buer (TLB)
Page Table RAM mein hoti hai. Har memory access ke liye RAM jana slow hai. Isliye CPU
mein ek specialized cache hota hai calledTLB. Yeh "Virtual to Physical" mapping ko cache
karta hai.   TLB Hit: Very fast. TLB Miss: Slow (Page table walk needed).


10.836           Elite Insight: The Math of TLB Reach
Dost, agar aapka working set (data jo program frequently use kar raha hai) TLB se bada
hai, toh system slow ho jayega chahe aapke paas kitni hi RAM ho.     Mathematical Formula:
TLB Reach = (TLB Entries) × (Page Size). Agar 1024 TLB entries hain aur 4KB page size,
             4MB hai. Aaj kal ke massive arrays GBs mein hote hain.
toh reach sirf
   Optimization: Performance engineers Huge Pages (2MB or 1GB) use karte hain taaki
TLB reach badh sake bina hardware change kiye.




10.837           Architecture Insights: Page Replacement Algorithms
Jab RAM full ho jaye aur naya page lana ho, toh kise nikalein (Evict)?


    LRU (Least Recently Used): Jo sabsse purana hai use nikalo.          (Best logic magar
       implement karna expensive hai).


    FIFO (First In First Out): Jo pehle aaya woh pehle gaya. (Simple par inecient -
       Belady's Anomaly).


    Clock Algorithm: LRU ka fast approximation jo modern kernels use karte hain.


10.838           Debugging Techniques: Swap Usage
Agar aapka Linux system `swap` bohot zada use kar raha hai (Check via `free -m` ya `top`),
toh iska matlab hai RAM kam pad gayi hai. System slow ho jayega kyunki Disk access speed
RAM se 1000x slow hoti hai. Isse   Thrashing kehte hain.

10.839           Exercise
1. Explain karo Belady's Anomaly kya hai? (Kyun frames badhane se page faults badh sakte
hain?). 2. Calculate karo: Agar Page Table mein 1 million entries hain aur har entry 4 bytes
ki hai, toh sirf page table kitni RAM legi?

Professional Engineering Series                                    Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




Common Mistakes
"Memory Mapped I/O" (mmap) ko ignore karna. Mmap aik powerful system call hai jo le ko
direct process ki virtual memory mein map kar deti hai bina `read()` calls ke. High-performance
databases (like MongoDB) iska use karte hain.




Introduction: Persistent Data Abstraction
Disk sirf raw sectors (0, 1, 2 . . . ) samajhti hai.   In sectors ko "Files" aur "Directories" mein
convert karna   File System ka kaam hai. Yeh darsal ek "Hierarchical Data Structure" hai jo
disk par rehta.




10.840          12.1 Files and Inodes
Linux/Unix mein le darsal ek     Inode hai.
    Inode (Index Node): Isme le ka metadata hota hai (Owner, Permissions, Size, Disk
      blocks ka address).


    Note: Inode mein "Filename" nahi hota! Filename directories mein store hota hai.


                                                  281
Professional Engineering Series                               Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




10.841        12.2 Directory Structure
Directory darsal aik special le hai jo "Filename -> Inode Number" ka map raski hai.




10.842        12.3 Operations: Open, Read, Write
Jab aap `open()` call karte hain: 1. OS directory search karta hai Inode dhoondhne ke liye. 2.
Inode check karke permissions verify hoti hain. 3. OS aik   File Descriptor (FD) wapis karta
hai (aik chota integer).




10.843        Architecture Insights: journaling File Systems
Agar computer le likhte waqt crash ho jaye, toh data corrupt ho sakta hai. Modern FS (Ext4,
NTFS)    Journaling use karte hain. Logic: Pehle "I am going to write this" log book mein
likho. Phir data likho. Phir log book clear karo. Agar beech mein crash hua, toh OS log book
dekh kar state recovery kar lega.




10.844        Real-World Examples
-Ext4: Linux standard. - NTFS: Windows standard (Advanced permissions/encryption). -
APFS: Apple's recent fast lesystem. - FAT32: Purana, simple magar limited (No 4GB+
les).




10.845        Debugging Techniques: df, du, fck
- `df -h`: Dekhne ke liye kitni space bachi hai aur kahan mounted hai. - `du -sh *`: Kaunsi
directory sabsse zada space le rahi hai. - `fsck`: File system internal errors x karne ke liye
tool.




10.846        Exercise
1. "Hard Link" aur "Soft Link" (Symbolic link) mein kya farq hai memory level par? 2. Kyun
empty les bhi disk par space leti hain? (Hint: Inodes).




Common Mistakes
"Too many open les" error. Har le descriptor aik kernel resource hai. Agar aap code mein
`close()` karna bhul jayenge, toh system limits hit kar jayega aur naye les open nahi honge.
Hamesha code mein `nally` block ya context managers use karein.




Introduction: Talking to Hardware
Disk access sabsse slow kaam hai computer mein. OS ka maqsad hai ki woh Disk ko ecient
banaye taaki users ko delay na feel ho.




                                             282
Professional Engineering Series                                Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




10.847        13.1 Disk Structure & Physicality
Purane HDD mein rotating platters aur physical arm hoti thi. Modern SSDs mein koi moving
part nahi hota, par internally woh pages aur blocks use karte hain.




10.848        13.2 Disk Scheduling Algorithms
HDD ke liye arms ki movement minimize karna zaroori hai:


    FCFS: Jaisa request aaya waisa arm move karo. (Worst performance).

    SSTF (Shortest Seek Time First): Agli woh request uthao jo arm ke sabsse kareeb
     hai. (Starvation ka khatra).


    SCAN (Elevator Algorithm): Arm aik side se doosri side scan karta hai, raste mein
     sab requests poori karta huwa. (Best for fairness).




10.849        13.3 Caching and Buering
OS RAM ka aik bada hissa    Page Cache ke liye use karta hai. - Read-ahead: Agar aap le
read kar rahe hain, toh OS uske agle blocks pehle hi RAM mein le aayega. - Write-back: Jab
aap le save karte hain, OS pehle RAM mein save karta hai aur baad mein (dheere se) disk
par likhta hai. Isse system fast lagta hai.




10.850        Architecture Insights: RAID (Redundant Array of
              Independent Disks)
                                         RAID 0 (Striping): Do disks ko mila kar speed
Zada performance aur data safety ke liye: -
double karna. (No safety). - RAID 1 (Mirroring): Data copy karna do disks par. (Full
safety, 1/2 space). - RAID 5: Speed aur Safety ka balance.




10.851        Debugging Techniques: iostat, iotop
- `iostat -x 1`: Dekhne ke liye disk queue length aur latency kitni hai. - `iotop`: Kaunsa process
sabsse zada Disk I/O (Read/Write) kar raha hai.




10.852        Exercise
1. SSD mein Disk Scheduling algorithms (jaise SCAN) ki zaroorat kyun nahi hoti? 2. "Hot
swapping" kya hoti hai server environments mein?




Common Mistakes
"Sync" na karna. Agar aap critical data likh rahe hain aur computer shutdown ho gaya, toh
`write()` call ke baad bhi data disk par nahi gaya hoga (Page Cache mein hoga). Critical apps
mein `fsync()` call karna zaroori hai.




                                               283
Professional Engineering Series                                Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




Introduction: The Bridge to World
Monitor, Mouse, Network Card, Printer  In sab ke hardware interfaces alag hain. OS in sab
ko manage karke aik standard "I/O Interface" deta hai.




10.853         14.1 Device Drivers
Device Driver aik software hai jo hardware-specic commands ko OS-understandable format
mein convert karta hai.


      Block Devices (Disk): Data xed blocks mein read/write hota hai. Random access
       possible.


      Character Devices (Keyboard, Serial): Data stream ke taur par aata hai. Random
       access impossible.




10.854         14.2 Interaction Models
1. Programmed I/O (Polling): CPU baar-baar hardware se puchta hai: "Kuch hai? Kuch
hai?". (CPU waste). 2. Interrupt-Driven I/O: Hardware OS ko signal bhejta hai (Interrupt)
jab data ready hota hai. (Most common). 3. Direct Memory Access (DMA): Hardware
CPU ko disturb kiye bina direct RAM mein data likh deta hai via specialized DMA controller.
(Maximum speed).




10.855         14.3 Interrupt Handling
                                                                   Interrupt Vector
Jab Interrupt aata hai: 1. CPU current task pause karta hai. 2. OS ke
Table (IVT) se handler function ka address uthata hai. 3. Interrupt Service Routine
(ISR) chalta hai. 4. Task resume hota hai.

10.856         Architecture Insights: Zero-Copy I/O
Normal networking mein: `Disk -> Kernel Buer -> User Buer -> Socket Buer -> Network
Card`. Yeh 4 bar data copy karna slow hai.    Zero-copy (`sendle` system call) use karke data
direct `Disk -> Kernel Buer -> Network Card` chala jata hai. High-scale servers (like Nginx,
Apache Kafka) isse use karte hain.




10.857         Debugging Techniques: lspci, lsusb
- `lspci`: PCI devices (GFX card, Network) list karne ke liye. - `lsusb`: Saare connected USB
devices dekhne ke liye.     - `/proc/interrupts`: Dekhne ke liye kaunsa device kitne interrupts
generate kar raha hai.




10.858         Exercise
1. "Interrupt Storm" kya hota hai aur OS isse kaise bachata hai? 2. Polling kab behter hai
Interrupts se? (Hint: High speed network packets).




                                               284
Professional Engineering Series                                Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




Common Mistakes
Interrupt handler (ISR) mein heavy kaam karna. ISR ko sabsse fast hona chahiye. Agar ISR
mein zada waqt lagayenge toh CPU hardware signals miss kar sakta hai.          Heavy kaam ko
hamesha "Bottom Half" (low priority task) mein move karein.




Introduction: Keeping the System Safe
OS ka sabsse bada challenge hai aik "Multi-user" environment mein system ko malicous apps
aur unauthorized users se bachana.




10.859         15.1 Authentication vs Authorization
-   Authentication: Kaun ho aap? (Login/Password). - Authorization: Kya aap yeh le
delete kar sakte hain? (Permissions).




10.860         15.2 Access Control Models
      DAC (Discretionary Access Control): File owner permission set karta hai (Unix
       style: `chmod 755`).


      MAC (Mandatory Access Control): System policy decide karti hai kaun kya kar
       sakta hai (SELinux, AppArmor). Even root user par restrictions lagai ja sakti hain.




10.861         15.3 Process Isolation
Har process apni virtual memory mein band hai.      Advanced Levels: - Namespaces: Process ko
lagta hai woh system par akela hai (Basis of Containers). - Cgroups: Process kitni RAM/CPU
use kar sakta hai, use limit karna.




10.862         Architecture Insights: Secure Enclaves (TEE)
Modern hardware (Intel SGX, ARM TrustZone) CPU ke andar aik "Secret Room" dete hain.
OS bhi use space ke andar ka data nahi dekh sakta. Secret keys aur bio-metric data yahan
store hota hai.




10.863         Real-World Attack: Buer Overow
Agar program user input verify nahi karta, toh attacker buer ke bahar write karke     Return
Address modify kar sakta hai aur apna malicious code run kar sakta hai. OS isse bachne
ke liye ASLR (Address Space Layout Randomization) use karta hai (Memory addresses
random jagah map karna).




10.864         Debugging Techniques: Auditd, Last
- `last`: System par pichle logins dekhne ke liye. - `auditd`: System calls track karne ke liye.
Kisi ne `/etc/passwd` touch kiya? Audit log bata dega.


                                              285
Professional Engineering Series                                 Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




10.865          Exercise
1. Explain karo `sudo` command kaise kaam karti hai internal levels par? (Hint: SUID bit). 2.
Kyun "Isolation" security ka pehla asool hai?




Common Mistakes
`root` user se apps chalana. Hamesha "Principle of Least Privilege" follow karein. Kisi app ko
sirf utni hi permission dein jitni zaroori hai. System service ko hamesha restricted user account
se chalayein.




Introduction: Computer inside Computer
Dost, Virtualization woh magic hai jisse hum ek physical computer par multiple OS (Windows,
Linux) aik saath chala sakte hain. Yeh modern "Cloud Computing" ki backbone hai.




10.866          16.1 The Hypervisor (VMM)
Hypervisor aik software layer hai jo hardware aur Virtual Machines (VMs) ke beech baithti
hai.


     Type 1 (Bare Metal): Direct hardware par chalta hai (ESXi, Xen).

     Type 2 (Hosted): Ek existing OS par chalta hai (VirtualBox, VMware Workstation).


10.867          16.2 Full Virtualization vs Para-virtualization
-Full Virtualization: Guest OS ko pata nahi hota woh VM mein hai. Woh hardware ko
                                                                          - Para-
direct access karne ki koshish karta hai aur Hypervisor signals translate karta hai.
virtualization: Guest OS ko pata hota hai woh VM mein hai. Woh Hypervisor ke sath
"Coordinate" karta hai via special system calls called Hypercalls.




10.868          16.3 Containerization (The Lightweight Alterna-
                tive)
VMs mein poora OS (Kernel + Apps) hota hai, jo bhari hai.           Containers (Docker) kernel
share karte hain aur sirf Apps/Libraries isolate karte hain.   Key Tech: Linux Namespaces aur
Cgroups.




10.869          Architecture Insights: Hardware-Assisted Virtual-
                ization
Modern CPUs mein    Intel VT-x ya AMD-V features hote hain. Yeh hypervisor ko permission
dete hain ki woh CPU par "Guest Mode" mein programs chala sake bina software interception
ke. Isse performance bohot badh jati hai.




                                              286
Professional Engineering Series                                 Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




10.870        Real-World Examples
-   AWS EC2: Nitro hypervisor use karta hai. - Docker/Kubernetes: Container orchestra-
tion ke liye standby tools.




10.871        Debugging Techniques: lscpu, dmesg
- `lscpu | grep Virt`: Dekhne ke liye CPU virtualization support karta hai ya nahi. - `/proc/cpuinfo`:
`vmx` ya `svm` ag dhoondhein.




10.872        Exercise
1. VM aur Container mein overhead ka kya dierence hai? 2. Kyun "Snapshots" virtualization
ka ek amazing feature hain?




Common Mistakes
Virtualization overhead ko ignore karna. Server par jitne zada VMs honge, utni context switch-
ing (VM exit/entry) badhegi. Mission-critical apps ke liye hum "Bare metal" prefer karte hain.




Introduction: Squeezing the Last Bit
OS internals ko optimize karna aik art hai. Goal hai ki har CPU cycle aur har byte RAM ka
maximum productive use ho.




10.873        17.1 CPU Optimization: Anity and Isolation
-CPU Anity: Ek process ko x core par bandh (bind) dena. Isse CPU Cache "warm" rehti
hai aur performance badh jati hai (`taskset` command). - Core Isolation: Linux kernel ko
bolna ki specic cores ko normal scheduling ke liye use na kare. Sirf aapka high-priority task
wahan chalega.




10.874        17.2 Memory Optimization: HugePages
Normal page size 4KB hota hai.      Agar RAM bohot zada hai, toh page table bohot badi ho
jati hai (TLB misses badhte hain).    Solution: HugePages (2MB ya 1GB blocks) use karna.
Databases like MySQL aur PostgreSQL isse performance boost ke liye use karte hain.




10.875        17.3 I/O Performance: Buers & Asynchronous
              I/O
-   AIO (Asynchronous I/O): Program `read()` call karta hai aur bhul jata hai. Jab data
                                                            Direct I/O: OS Page Cache
ready hota hai, OS callback bhejta hai. CPU idle nahi baithta. -
ko bypass karke direct disk se RAM mein data copy karna (Used by Databases for custom
caching).




                                               287
Professional Engineering Series                                Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




10.876        Architecture Insights: NUMA (Non-Uniform Mem-
              ory Access)
Modern servers mein multiple CPUs hote hain. Har CPU ki apni "Local RAM" hoti hai. Agar
CPU 1, CPU 2 ki RAM access karega, toh delay aayega. High-performance software       NUMA
aware hona chahiye taaki CPU hamesha apni local RAM use kare.

10.877        Debugging Techniques: Perf, Valgrind
- `perf top`: Live system hotspots dekhne ke liye (kaunsa function sabsse zada CPU le raha
hai). - `perf stat`: CPU cycles, instructions, cache misses count karne ke liye.




10.878        Exercise
1. HugePages se "TLB Miss" kaise kam hote hain? 2. "Context switch" cost ko optimize karne
ke liye "User-space threads" (Fibers) kaise help karte hain?




Common Mistakes
Early optimization.   Pehle check karein "Bottleneck" kahan hai (`perf ` use karein).   Aksar
bottleneck Disk I/O hota hai magar developers CPU code optimize karte reh jate hain.




Introduction: Operating Systems without Borders
Distributed OS aik aisa system hai jo multiple independent computers (nodes) par chalta hai
par user ko lagta hai woh aik hi system use kar raha hai.




10.879        18.1 Resource Management
-Transparency: User ko nahi pata resource local hai ya remote. - Scalability: Naye nodes
add karne se capability badh jaye. - Fault Tolerance: Agar ek server crash ho jaye, toh
doosra kaam sambhaal le.




10.880        18.2 Coordination: Clock Synchronization
Distributed systems mein "Waqt" (Time) sabsse bada challenge hai. Har node ki clock alag ho
         Algorithms: - NTP (Network Time Protocol). - Logical Clocks (Lamport
sakti hai.
Clocks): Sirf event order track karna, real time nahi.

10.881        18.3 Remote Procedure Call (RPC)
Aap aik function call karte hain, magar woh darsal doosre server par execute hota hai.    OS
network latency aur serialization ko hide karta hai.




                                              288
Professional Engineering Series                                  Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




10.882          Architecture Insights: Distributed Shared Memory
                (DSM)
Aik aisa illusion jahan saare servers ki RAM mil kar aik bada address space banati hai. Yeh
implement karna bohot mushkil hai "Consistency" issues ki wajah se.




10.883          Real-World Examples
-   Google Cluster OS (Borg): Jo ab Kubernetes ban chuka hai. - Apache Spark: Dis-
tributed memory processing ke liye.




10.884          Debugging Techniques: Ping, Traceroute, Logs
- `tcpdump`: Network level par packets inspect karne ke liye.       - Centralized Logging (ELK
stack): Sab servers ke logs aik jagah dekhne ke liye.




10.885          Exercise
1.   Distributed system mein "Race conditions" synchronization level par kaise manage hoti
hain?     (Hint: Distributed Locks like Redis/Zookeeper).   2.   Distributed OS aur Network OS
(Simple client-server) mein kya farq hai?




Common Mistakes
Network failure ko ignore karna. Distributed OS mein network hamesha unreliable hota hai.
Hamesha "Retries" aur "Timeouts" logic code mein hona chahiye.




Introduction: Case Studies of Giants
Dost, theory apni jagah, par real-world OS ne bahut dierent design decisions liye hain. Aaj
hum Linux, Windows, aur macOS ke architectures ko compare karenge.




10.886          19.1 Linux: The Monolithic Powerhouse
-Kernel: Pure Monolithic (magar modular). Loadable Kernel Modules (LKM) se drivers
on-the-y add/remove ho sakte hain. - Strength: Open source, highly customizable, server
dominance. - Design Philosophy: "Everything is a le" (Files, Sockets, Pipes sab les hain).




10.887          19.2 Windows: The Hybrid Giant
-Kernel: Windows NT (Hybrid). - Architecture: Client-Server model use hota hai kernel
mein. User-mode "Subsystems" (Win32, Linux/WSL) kernel se baat karte hain. - Strength:
Backward compatibility aur GUI integration. Hardware drivers ke liye strict "Driver signing"
policy.




                                              289
Professional Engineering Series                              Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




10.888        19.3 macOS (Darwin/XNU): The Unix Beauty
-Kernel: XNU (X is Not Unix). - Architecture: Hybrid kernel jo Mach microkernel aur BSD
(Unix) capabilities ko combine karta hai. - Strength: Graphics and Multimedia performance.
High security via sandboxing.




10.889        Deep Dive: Executable Formats
-Linux: ELF (Executable and Linkable Format). - Windows: PE (Portable Executable). -
macOS: Mach-O. In formats mein instruction code ke sath sath "Header" aur "Symbol Table"
hoti hai jo OS loader ko batati hai ki program kaise load karna hai.




10.890        Engineering Insights: Boot Process
1. BIOS/UEFI: Hardware check (POST). 2. Bootloader (GRUB/Windows Boot Man-
ager): Kernel ko RAM mein load karta hai. 3. Kernel Initialization: Drivers load hote
hain, Scheduler start hota hai. 4. Init/Systemd: Pehla user process (PID 1) chalta hai jo
baqi services start karta hai.




10.891        Exercise
1.   Kyun Linux servers ke liye sabsse best maana jata hai?     2.     Explain karo Linux mein
"Everything is a le" kyun ek masterstroke hai API design mein?




Common Mistakes
Windows concepts ko Linux par apply karna. Jaise Linux mein process create karna (`fork`)
sasta hai, par Windows mein `CreateProcess` mehnga hai. Windows developers threads prefer
karte hain, Linux developers processes se nahi darte.




Introduction: Build Your Own OS Logic
Theory aur internals seekhne ke baad, waqt hai unhe code mein dekhne ka. Hum three sim-
plied mini-projects karenge.




10.892        20.1 Project 1: Simple Round Robin Scheduler
Goal: Simulation likhna jo multiple tasks ko xed time slice mein chalaye.
tasks = [{ " id " : 1, " time " : 50} , { " id ": 2 , " time ": 30}]
quantum = 20

while tasks :
    t = tasks . pop (0)
    run \ _time = min (t[" time "] , quantum )
    t[ " time " ] -= run \ _time
    print (f " Running Task {t[ ' id ']} for { run \ _time } ms ")
    if t[" time "] > 0:

                                             290
Professional Engineering Series                             Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




           tasks . append ( t)



10.893        20.2 Project 2: Virtual Memory simulation
Goal: Page Table map karna.
page \ _table = {0: 10 , 1: 25} # Virtual Page -> Physical Frame
PAGE \ _SIZE = 4096

def translate ( virtual \ _addr ):
    page \ _num = virtual \ _addr // PAGE \ _SIZE
    offset = virtual \ _addr % PAGE \ _SIZE
    if page \ _num in page \ _table :
         return ( page \ _table [ page \ _num ] * PAGE \ _SIZE ) + offset
    else :
         return " PAGE FAULT ! "



10.894        20.3 Project 3: Simple File System (Inode style)
Goal: File metadata aur block storage manage karna.
inodes = {
    1: {" name " : " test . txt " , " blocks ": [100 , 101] , " size ": 8192}
}
disk = {100: " Hello " , 101: " World !"}

def read \ _file ( inode \ _id ):
    data = ""
    for b in inodes [ inode \ _id ][ " blocks " ]:
         data += disk [ b]
    return data



10.895        Engineering Decisions Step-by-Step
1.   Scheduler: Fairness choose ki (Round Robin) over complexity. 2. Memory: Fixed size
paging rakhi fragmentation bachane ke liye. 3. FS: Inodes use kiye taaki le blocks non-
contiguous ho sakein (eciency).




10.896        Final Graduation: You are a Systems Engineer!
Dost, OS aik zinda machine hai jo constant optimization mangti hai.       Aapne Seekha:    1.
User/Kernel Isolation.   2.   Process/Thread management.   3.   RAM aur Virtual Memory ka
jadu. 4. Filesystems aur I/O connectivity. Hamesha system logs (`dmesg`, `journalctl`) padhte
rahein, wohi system ki asli kahani batate hain.

10.897         Mastery: Process Scheduling & IPC (Inter-Process
               Communication)
10.897.1 1. Problem Motivation
Dost, sochiye aapka computer aik "Single CPU" machine hai.             The Challenge: 1. The
Multitasking Illusion: Aap aik hi waqt mein Music sun rahe hain, Chrome browser chal
raha hai, aur background mein antivirus scan ho raha hai. CPU aik milisecond mein sirf aik
kaam kar sakta hai.      Ye sab aik saath kaise chal rahe hain?   2.    The Isolation Barrier:
Aik process (like Excel) doosre process (like Python Script) ka data direct nahi dekh sakta
(Memory protection).     Magar kya ho agar "Excel" ko Python se data lena ho?      Humein aik
aisa "Time Manager" (Scheduler) chahiye jo CPU ka time sabko baante, aur aik "Telegram
System" (IPC) jo in isolated processes ko baat karne daitay.



10.897.2 2. Intuition Explanation
Scheduling aur IPC ko "The Shared Oce" model samjhein:


      The Scheduler (The Oce Manager): Sochiye aik hi computer (CPU) hai aur 10
       log kaam karna chahte hain.    Manager decide karta hai: "Tum 2 min kaam karo, phir
       tumhari bari."


      Pre-emption (The Priority Interruption): Agar CEO (High Priority process) aata
       hai, toh manager employee ko hata kar foran CEO ko seat daita hai.


      IPC (The Comm Mechanisms):             - Shared Memory (The Common White-
       board): Dono processes aik hi jagah likhte aur padhte hain. Fast hai, magar "Safety"
       (locks) ka dhayan rakhna padta hai. - Message Queues (The Inter-oce Mail): Ek
       process mail bhejta hai, doosra jab free ho tab padhta hai. Slow magar safe. - Pipes
       (The Water Pipe): One-way ow of data.

10.897.3 3. Technical Denition
      Process: A program in execution.

      Scheduler: OS ka woh part jo decide karta hai ki Ready queue mein se kaunsa process
       CPU par jayega.


      Context Switch: Ek process ki state (Registers, PC) save karke doosre process ki state
       load karna.


      IPC (Inter-Process Communication): Mechanisms (Pipes, Sockets, Signals, Shared
       Memory) jisse processes data aur coordination exchange karte hain.




                                              292
Professional Engineering Series                             Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




10.897.4 4. Internal Working: The Scheduling Algorithms Mastery
1. Scheduling Strategies: - First-Come-First-Serve (FCFS): Jo pehle aaya usey CPU
mila. Problem: "Convoy Eect" (Badi process piche saari choti processes ko block kar daiti
hai). -Round Robin (RR): Har process ko aik xed "Time Quantum" (e.g. 10ms) milta hai.
Fair hai magar context switching overhead badha sakta hai. - Multi-Level Feedback Queue
(MLFQ): Modern OS like Linux/Windows ise use karte hain. Ye processes ko "Behavior"
(Interactive vs CPU-bound) ke hisab se dynamically prioritize karta hai.
   2. IPC Mechanics: - Signals: Chote notications (e.g. SIGKILL, SIGTERM). - Sockets:
                                    Semaphore/Mutex: IPC ke doran "Wait/Signal"
Local ya Network across communication. -
logic manage karna (Race conditions rokne ke liye).



10.897.5 5. Architecture or Flow
graph TD
    P1 [ Process 1] -- Shared Memory --> Shared [( RAM Segment )]
    Shared -- Read / Write --> P2 [ Process 2]
    P1 -- Pipe --> P2
    subgraph " Scheduler Logic "
    ReadyQ [ Ready Queue ] --> Scheduler [ OS Scheduler ]
    Scheduler - ->| Assign CPU | Running [ Running Process ]
    Running -- Time Slice Over --> ReadyQ
    end
                            Listing 10.48: Architecture Diagram




10.897.6 6. Code Example / OS Programming Mastery
// 1. Creating a child process ( Fork )
# include < unistd .h >
int pid = fork () ;
if ( pid == 0) {
     // This is the Child Process
} else {
     // This is the Parent Process
}

// 2. Simple Pipe IPC
int pipefds [2];
char buffer [20];
pipe ( pipefds );
if ( fork () == 0) {
     write ( pipefds [1] , " Hello Parent " , 12) ; // Send data
} else {
     read ( pipefds [0] , buffer , 12) ; // Receive data
     printf (" Received : %s\ n" , buffer );
}

// 3. Round Robin Visualization ( Conceptual )
// Queue : [P1 , P2 , P3 ] -> ( Time Quantum 10 ms )
// P1 runs 10 ms -> [P2 , P3 , P1 ] -> P2 runs 10 ms ...



                                            293
Professional Engineering Series                                Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




10.897.7 7. Complexity Analysis
      Scheduling: Perfect scheduling O(1) lookup mangti hai Ready Queue se. Linux ka CFS
       (Completely Fair Scheduler) Red-Black tree use karta hai jiske complexity O(log N ) hai.


      Context Switch:         Ye "Pure Overhead" hai.   Isme spent kiya gaya time asali kaam
       (Utility) mein nahi gina jata.



10.897.8 8. Real-World Usage
      Web Servers (Nginx): Worker processes ke beech IPC use kar ke load balance karte
       hain.


      Browsers (Chrome): Har Tab aik alag process hai (for security). Wo main frame se
       IPC ke zariye baat karte hain.


      Docker: Containers actually host kernel ki scheduling aur isolation (namespaces) use
       karte hain.



10.897.9 9. Trade-os
      Pros of IPC: Parallelism (Aik bada kaam multiple processes mein todna). Security
       (Independent processes crash isolated rehte hain).


      Cons: - Complexity: Shared memory mein "Deadlocks" aur "Race Conditions" handle
       karna bhot mushkil hai. -    Performance: Message passing mein data copy karna padta
       hai (O(N ) data size).



10.897.10 10. Mastery Challenge Questions
1.   "Process" aur "Thread" mein context switching overhead ka kya farq hai?          2.   "Zombie
Process" aur "Orphan Process" kya hote hain? 3. "Priority Inversion" problem kya hai aur
"Priority Inheritance" isse kaisey theek karti hai? 4. Linux ka Completely      Fair Scheduler
(CFS) "Virtual Runtime" kaise calculate karta hai? 5. How would you implement a "Producer-
Consumer" problem using Semaphores? 6. Why are "Unnamed Pipes" limited to parent-child
processes? (Hint: Shared le descriptors). 7. Describe the "Shared Memory" lifecycle (shmget,
shmat, shmdt).       8.   What is the impact of a very small "Time Quantum" in Round Robin
scheduling?    9.    Explain "Soft Real-time" vs "Hard Real-time" scheduling.   10.   How to use
ipcs and ipcrm commands in Linux to manage IPC resources?


10.898         Professional Engineering Perspective
10.898.1 1. Edge Cases
      Starvation in Priority Scheduling:: Agar musalsal high-priority processes aati rahein,
       toh low-priority process kabhi nahi chal sakti. Solution: "Aging" (Time ke sath priority
       badhana).


      Race Condition in Shared Memory: Agar do processes aik aik variable counter++
       kar rahe hain bina lock ke, toh nal value inconsistent hogi. Synchronization primitives
       like semaphores are non-negotiable here.




                                               294
Professional Engineering Series                               Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




10.898.2 2. Production Failure Example
The Kafka IPC Bottleneck:: Ek massive scale pipeline mein producer aur consumer same
machine par thay magar standard TCP sockets use kar rahe thay.        Latency high thi kyonki
Linux kernel ko data multiple times copy karna padta hai. Jab logic "Unix Domain Sockets"
(Local IPC) par move hua, throughput 3x barh gaya. Lesson: Local scaling ke liye network
stack overhead bypass karein.



10.898.3 3. Debugging Strategy
      htop / top: Live scheduling monitor karein (PRI, NI columns).

      strace -e trace=ipc: Sirf IPC related system calls (msgget, semop, shmget) track karein
       problem pinpoint karne ke liye.



10.898.4 4. Interview Corner

       ing?



10.898.5 5. Engineering Insight: "Cooperation is Faster than Isola-
         tion"
Dost, OS darsal aik "Shared Economy" hai. Senior engineers hamesha "Lockless" IPC (like
Ring Buers) aur ecient scheduling settings (nice,      ionice) ka use karte hain system ko
smooth rakhne ke liye. Coordination design karna mushkil hai, magar performant systems ke
liye lazmi hai.   - [x] Mastery 02: Process Scheduling - Appended Professional Sections - [ ]

10.899         Mastery: Memory Management (Paging & Virtual
               Memory)
10.899.1 1. Problem Motivation
Dost, sochiye aapke computer mein sirf 8GB RAM hai.         The Challenge: 1. The Over-
crowding: Aapne Chrome chalaya (2GB), Photoshop chalaya (4GB), aur aik game khol li
(6GB). Total 12GB memory chahiye magar hai sirf 8GB. Computer crash kyun nahi hota?
2.   The Security: Kya ho agar aik virus-app doosri app (like Bank app) ki RAM ke andar
                        The Fragmentation: Agar aapne bhot saari apps kholi aur band
read/write karne lage? 3.
ki, toh RAM mein chhote-chhote "Khali Slots" (Holes) ban jayenge jahan koi nayi badi app
t nahi aayegi. Computer Science mein iska solution hai    Virtual Memory aur Paging. Ye
darsal "Ankhon ka Dhoka" hai jo har app ko lagta hai ki uske paas "Innite" aur "Private"
memory hai.




                                              295
Professional Engineering Series                             Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




10.899.2 2. Intuition Explanation
Virtual Memory ko "The Library Card" model samjhein:


    The Virtual Address (The Catalog Number): Sochiye aapko aik book chahiye.
     Library ke catalog mein uska number A-101 hai. Ye aapka "Virtual" address hai.


    The Physical RAM (The Actual Shelf): Asali shelf par book shayad Basement mein
     rakhi ho (Physical Address).


    The Page Table (The Librarian): Libraray ki librarian (Page Table) ke paas aik
     register hai jo batata hai ki Catalog Number A-101 asaliyat mein kis shelf (Physical
     Frame) par hai.


    Paging (Fixed Size Boxes): Hum memory ko equal sizes ke "Pages" (usually 4KB)
     mein baant dete hain. Isse management asaan ho jati hai aur fragmentation khatam hoti
     hai.


    Swap Space (The Warehouse): Agar RAM full ho jaye, toh hum kam use hone wale
     "Pages" ko Hard Disk (Swap) mein bhej daitay hain (Demand Paging).



10.899.3 3. Technical Denition
    Virtual Memory: Ek abstract technique jo physical RAM aur disk storage ko mila kar
     ek bada memory space create karti hai.


    MMU (Memory Management Unit): Hardware component jo Virtual addresses ko
     Physical addresses mein convert karta hai.


    Page Table: Kernel ka data structure jahan Mapping (Virtual -> Physical) store hoti
     hai.


    Page Fault: Jab CPU aisi memory mangta hai jo RAM mein nahi hai (Disk par hai),
     toh OS usey disk se uthakar lata hai.



10.899.4 4. Internal Working: The Address Translation Mastery
1. The Translation Process: - CPU ek Virtual Address bhejta hai (e.g., 0x1234). - MMU
Page Table mein dekhta hai: "Page #1 kahan hai?". - Agar Page RAM mein hai (Valid bit
1), toh mapping return hoti hai. - Agar Valid bit 0 hai → Page Fault. - OS disk se data laya
→ RAM mein rakha → Page Table update kiya.
   2. TLB (Translation Lookaside Buer): - Har bar Page Table (RAM mein) dekhna
slow hai. - CPU ke paas aik "Cache" hota hai (TLB) jo sabsse zada use hone wali mappings
ko O(1) mein dhoondh leta hai.
   3. Hierarchical Paging: - Modern systems mein Page Table bhot bada ho jata hai. Isliye
hum "Multi-level Page Tables" use karte hain (Tree structure).



10.899.5 5. Architecture or Flow
graph LR
    CPU [ CPU : Virtual Address ] --> MMU [ MMU Controller ]
    MMU --> TLB { TLB Hit ?}
    TLB -- Yes --> RAM [ Physical RAM Frame ]

                                              296
Professional Engineering Series                                 Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




      TLB -- No --> PT [ Page Table in RAM ]
      PT -- Found --> RAM
      PT -- Not Found --> PF [ Page Fault Handler ]
      PF --> Disk [( Swap / Disk )]
      Disk --> RAM
                              Listing 10.49: Architecture Diagram




10.899.6 6. Code Example / Memory Mastery
// 1. Allocating Virtual Memory in C
# include < stdlib .h >
int * ptr = ( int *) malloc (1024 * sizeof ( int ) );
// At this point , the OS has not given real RAM yet ! ( Lazy Allocation )
// RAM will be allocated only when you ' write ' to it .

//   2. Monitoring Page Faults ( Linux )
//   ps -o min \ _flt , maj \ _flt -p <pid >
//   Major Fault = Data taken from Disk ( Slow )
//   Minor Fault = Mapping done in RAM ( Fast )

// 3. Memory Mapping ( mmap )
// Directly mapping a file to memory ( Virtual Space )
void * addr = mmap ( NULL , size , PROT \ _READ , MAP \ _PRIVATE , fd , 0) ;


10.899.7 7. Complexity Analysis
      Time Complexity: TLB hit par address translation near O(1) (Hardware speed). Page
       Fault par latency O(Disk _Seek) ho jati hai (Very slow).


      Space Complexity: Page tables khud RAM consume karte hain. Multi-level paging
       isse optimize karti hai magar translation steps badha deti hai.



10.899.8 8. Real-World Usage
      Security: Har process ki Page Table alag hoti hai, isliye Process A kabhi Process B ki
       memory nahi dekh sakta.


      Shared Memory: Do processes ko aik hi "Physical Frame" map karke fast communi-
       cation (IPC) karwayi jati hai.


      Performance: Demand paging ki wajah se hi hum 100GB ki games 16GB RAM par
       khel sakte hain.



10.899.9 9. Trade-os
      Pros: Innite-like Memory (Disk abstraction). Universal Addressing (Same code
       runs regardless of RAM size).    Security.
      Cons: - Thrashing: Agar RAM bhot kam hai aur Page Faults bhot zada, toh CPU sirf
       data "Swap" karne mein lag jata hai aur asali kaam (Utility) zero ho jata hai. -   Latency:
       TLB misses performance gira sakte hain.


                                               297
Professional Engineering Series                                Ultimate Mastery Guide (10/10)




10.899.10 10. Mastery Challenge Questions
1. "Internal Fragmentation" aur "External Fragmentation" mein kya dierence hai aur paging
isse kaisey solve karti hai?   2.   "Thrashing" ko detect aur prevent kaise kiya jata hai?   3.
"Copy-on-Write" (COW) fork() syscall mein memory kaisey bachata hai? 4. Virtual address
32-bit vs 64-bit mein page table size par kya asar hota hai? 5. How would you design a "Page
Replacement Algorithm" (e.g.        LRU vs FIFO)? 6.    Why is the TLB (Translation Lookaside

architectures. 10. How to use /proc/self/maps in Linux to see the virtual memory layout of
